Label: Access Rights
Usage notes: Either "public" or "restricted"
Crosswalk notes: if idinfo/accconst is "None" then "public" else "restricted"

Label: Alternative Title
Usage notes: This is new, might have applicability for datasets with other titles, including titles we have changed to meet our standard formats.  I think this will be useful for new datasets that we add and can keep the original title here.  I am not inclined to try and retcon existing collections however.
Crosswalk notes: NA

Label: Bounding Box
Usage notes: Defined by the geographic extent of the dataset.  Can use the bounding box extent as found in the geometry.  However, this extent is used for geographic searching and there may be situations in which altering the bounding box might be better for discovery. (One example are US datasets that include data for Guam, making the data functionally world coverage - it may be better to limit to state level extents).
Crosswalk notes: All xml records have valid entry

Label: Centroid
Usage notes: While we could calculate this from bounding box, not sure that we need the functionality at the moment.  Leaning towards not using
Crosswalk notes: NA

Label: Creator
Usage notes: There should be a minimum of one of these in every record, multiple entries are fine.  Use entries from the Library of Congress Name Authority File if at all possible.
Crosswalk notes: Multi-value; collect all <origin> tags from the primary <citeinfo> block (not from <lworkcit>).  We have remediated all of these to LC names as of 7/2026

Label: Date Issued
Usage notes: This should be the date of publication NOT the date represented in the data.  Keep this is the YYYY single year formt. If published over a range, typically use final year.  Date ranges not forbidden by Aardvark standard but should be exceptional.
Crosswalk notes: All updated to YYYY format as of 7/2026

Label: Date Range
Usage notes: We have a few dozen records that use this field and we may implement down the road.  Only use if date ranges are essential and cannot reasonably be used in single date formats.  Default to YYYY to YYYY format unless narrower dates are necessary (Aarvark does not support without inhouse customization, but we may add that in the future).  Regardless this is not a replacement for Temporal Coverage
Crosswalk notes: FGDC did not allow for single date and date range period both to be used in same record.  But Aarvark does.  So we may as well include this.   GeoBlacklight suggests a format in array that includes brackets: "[YYYY TO YYYY]"  all relevant entries are already in YYYY pair formats

Label: Description
Usage notes: Single value; wrap in array. May contain HTML in some records - these can be enables in GeoBlacklight.  Any text can be included here and all will display on landing record.  Typically, we employ a first sentence that states the title and the data format, representation, geography and date.  For example: "New York City NYPD Arrest Data, 2006 is a point theme representing every New York Police Department Arrest in the year 2006."  Usually followed by a few sentences given overview information about the dataset - typical things to include are significant components of the attribute data, source material information, data format and projection.  If CUL staff did meaningful transformation of the data include a note to that effect as well.
Crosswalk notes: All xml records have valid entry

Label: Display Note
Source (FGDC XPath / Value): NA - not currently used, however relevent info may be in abtract.
Usage notes: Entries in this field will be displayed in a colored box on the GeoBlacklight Landing page.  Will only be used in particular circumstances for specific messages.  For Example, for datasets that cannot be downloaded remotely.
Crosswalk notes: Will remediate after transfer

Label: File Size
Usage notes: We will start using this again now that it is supported but Aardvark.  Enter file size in megabytes - example "25.96 MB". Use approximate size of file before compressing - some files will be taken directly from PostGres and the direct download will be smaller when compressed.  So this won't really be accurate, but good to start working with.
Crosswalk notes: These have been remediated as of 7/2026 and the exact value can be used.  Text entry with "MB" indicator as suggested by Aardvark.

Label: Format
Usage notes: Has a recommended vocabulary - https://opengeometadata.org/ogm-aardvark/#format.  Usually will be "GeoPackage" or "GeoTIFF" or "TIFF"
Crosswalk notes: All FDGC records in compliance with recommended vocabulary of  7/2026

Label: Geometry
Usage notes: Defined by the geographic extent of the dataset.  Can use the bounding box extent as found in the geometry.  However, this extent is used for geographic searching and there may be situations in which altering the bounding box might be better for discovery. (One example are US datasets that include data for Guam, making the data functionally world coverage - it may be better to limit to state level extents).
Crosswalk notes: Convert to WKT ENVELOPE: ENVELOPE(westbc, eastbc, northbc, southbc). Note WKT ENVELOPE argument order is (minX, maxX, maxY, minY) = (west, east, north, south). Validate bounds are within ±180/±90.

Label: Georeferenced
Usage notes: Simple indicator for georeferenced data.  Will almost always be "true"  some tabular data or scanned maps may be "false"
Crosswalk notes: If spref block is  present then "true" else "false"  All record remediated for this as of 7/2026

Label: Identifier
Usage notes: created by us, also used as filename.  Standard format is to create a short code identifying the provider/publisher and to use that at beginning of filename. Format typically is publisher_place_feature_year all lower case.  Examples; landinfo_london_roads_2010  leaddog_mexico_census_2007
Crosswalk notes: All present and valid

Label: Identifier
Usage notes: New and can provide alternative identifier for other purposes, such as a DOI.  CIESIN collections would be good for this
Crosswalk notes: Will remediate after transfer

Label: Index Year
Usage notes: Integer year for faceting. Thus this most important single year for describing the dataset.  Use date that the data represents.  Use YYYY value only.  For date ranges, can use multiple values. Fall back to pubdate if caldate absent.
Crosswalk notes: For files that use rngdates, enter every year in YYYY format inclusive.

Label: Is Part Of
Usage notes: This is specifically for a "subset of another item" and that bigger item should have it's own record. Not sure that we have reasonable use for this in lieu of "member of " field
Crosswalk notes: NA - we will not use for now

Label: Is Replaced By
Source (FGDC XPath / Value): NA - no data for this at present
Usage notes: This is new - a solution for records that are superseded.  Historically we have removed these records (ESRI) but this may open up opportunity to keep in in the future.
Crosswalk notes: Will remediate after transfer

Label: Is Version Of
Source (FGDC XPath / Value): NA - no data for this at present
Usage notes: This is new - a solution for records that are repeated.  Consider.
Crosswalk notes: Will remediate after transfer

Label: Keyword
Usage notes: This is new and for subject keywords that are not seen but are indexed for searching.  Almost all records have uncontrolled subject keywords that can come here.  Add freely but thoughtfully.
Crosswalk notes: All theme keywords that have themekt="None" can be placed here.  Any controlled vocabularies go in Theme or Subject

Label: Language
Usage notes: Aardvark expects ISO 639-2 codes, nearly all records should get an "eng" entry but may datasets may have components that are in other languages.  We will use this field now and can employ multiple languages
Crosswalk notes: idinfo/descript/langdata is an extension for language data.  There is at least one value for every record, some have two. Include all.

Label: License
Source (FGDC XPath / Value): NA - no data for this at present
Usage notes: This is new and can provide a uri to licenses information.  Can investigate for future use.,
Crosswalk notes: NA

Label: Member Of
Source (FGDC XPath / Value): NA - no data for this at present
Usage notes: This is not something we have used before and can allow collection level description.  Of all the new fields that allow relationship descriptions, this has the most promise for us and we should start to build collections around it. Should be implemented and needs remediation.  Aardvark recommends the creation of a collection record and the id of the record to be the entry here.  So we cannot do this till aardvark is implemented.  PRIORITY at that time.
Crosswalk notes: Will remediate after transfer

Label: Metadata Version
Usage notes: Always "Aardvark". Hardcode.
Crosswalk notes: All values should be "Aardvark"

Label: Modified
Source (FGDC XPath / Value): NA - we will enter curent date
Usage notes: Enter the date this record is created, use  ISO 8601 datetime: YYYY-MM-DDT00:00:00Z.
Crosswalk notes: FGDC format is YYYYMMDD; convert to W3C Date and Time Format: YYYY-MM-DDT00:00:00Z.  This should be updated when metadata is modified so enter current date and time.

Label: Provider
Usage notes: Always "Columbia". Hardcode.
Crosswalk notes: All values should be "Columbia"

Label: Publisher
Usage notes: Single publisher name. Recommended vocabulary is LOC Names NOT the publisher entry as used on materials.
Crosswalk notes: All records remediated as of 7/2026.  One publisher entry from LC name authorities.

Label: References (Links)
Usage notes: DIRECT DOWNLOAD LINK - References is where all links can go, currently there is one of these for direct download for almost all records.
Crosswalk notes: All records remediated. One and only one link for almost all records.

Label: References (Links)
Source (FGDC XPath / Value): Construct from GBL base URL + record ID
Usage notes: Can make a link also to Geodata landing page.  "https://geodata.library.columbia.edu/catalog/"  followed buy the record ID.
Crosswalk notes: This can be constructed by the GeoData landing page link "https://geodata.library.columbia.edu/catalog/"  followed by the record ID.

Label: References (Links)
Usage notes: WFS endpoint, can be hardcoded and combined with WxS identified field.
Crosswalk notes: All values should be "https://geoserver.cul.columbia.edu/geoserver/sde/ows"  Only include if access rights value is "public" otherwise blank

Label: References (Links)
Usage notes: WMS endpoint, can be hardcoded and combined with WxS identified field.
Crosswalk notes: All values should be "https://geoserver.cul.columbia.edu/geoserver/wms/sde"  Only include if access rights value is "public" otherwise blank

Label: Relation
Source (FGDC XPath / Value): NA - no data for this at present
Usage notes: Can create relations that are more general than other option allow.  Does not require a separate creation of a "collection" record. Could have some utility for us.  Consider.
Crosswalk notes: Data for this can be found in /metadata/idinfo/crossref/citeinfo but there is no id in here to input.  Also many of these will not be in our collection.  So we will ignore

Label: Replaces
Source (FGDC XPath / Value): NA - no data for this at present
Usage notes: This is new - a solution for records that are superseded.  Companion to "Is replaced by" but maybe more useful and easier to implement if we don’t surface older editions.  Consider
Crosswalk notes: Will remediate after transfer

Label: Resource Class
Usage notes: New field and required.  There is a recommended list of terms, most of ours will be "Datasets" or "Maps".  Will need to hardcode.  Will investigate
Crosswalk notes: Will remediate after transfer

Label: Resource Type
Usage notes: Most of our collection will be one of "Point data", "Line data", "Polygon data", or "Raster data".  See the reccomended vocabularies for alternate cases.
Crosswalk notes: Most of our collection will be one of "Point data", "Line data", "Polygon data", or "Raster data".  Here is the logic: if spdoinfo\ptvctinf\sdtsterm\sdtstype = "String" then "Line data".  if spdoinfo\ptvctinf\sdtsterm\sdtstype = "G-polygon" then "Polygon data".   if spdoinfo\ptvctinf\sdtsterm\sdtstype = "Entity point" then "Point data".  if spdoinfo\direct = "Raster" then "Raster data".   Some of the raster data and misc types will need remediated after transfer

Label: Rights
Usage notes: Free text; use as-is. Consider supplementing with standard Columbia rights statement for public domain records.
Crosswalk notes: Free text; use as-is.

Label: Rights Holder
Usage notes: Use LC names for this as recommended by Aardvark.  Usually will be same as origin.
Crosswalk notes: All updated and in accordance with LC names recommendation

Label: Source
Usage notes: Source datasets used. Use record IDs if sources are in the collection.  We don't have records of these source materials and probably will not employ for now.  For works that are wholly derived from another (like the scanned maps). There is some information on this in the xml that you can take from the dataqual/lineage/srcinfo/srccite/citeinfo/title.  However, these are not all applicable nor do they have records that we can include ID for.  Some may have other IDs we can link to (like CLIO records for maps or other DOIs) but this is low priority and can be tabled for now.  I have made a table of all extant entries we can revisit later.
Crosswalk notes: Some dataqual/lineage/srcinfo/srccite/citeinfo/title entries are relevant, but we will not use for now and  will need remediated after transfer.  Ignore for now

Label: Spatial Coverage
Usage notes: Geonames recommended here and we have added one for all.  Recommendations are for largest extent with a preference for nations - done. Other controlled and uncontrolled terms are in almost every record and we can keep all.
Crosswalk notes: Collect all <placekey> values across all <place> blocks.

Label: Subject
Usage notes: Controlled vocabularies are encouraged but not required.  We have many uncontrolled keywords along with LC and a variety of others.  No real GeoBlacklight standard other than Iso which will go in Theme.  Uncontrolled subjects will go in keyword where indexed but not displayed.  These can be displayed if needed.  Using LC subjects fine but not required, use judgement but fine to leave blank.
Crosswalk notes: Any themekey from non-ISO or uncontrolled vocabularies can go here.

Label: Suppressed
Usage notes: Default; set to true for records under embargo
Crosswalk notes: Set to "FALSE" for all

Label: Temporal Coverage
Source (FGDC XPath / Value): NA - not used in xml records
Usage notes: This is a text string that can indicate uncertainty.  Use this for dates that are not suitably captured by the YYYY entries in the Index Year field.
Crosswalk notes: Ignore for now.

Label: Theme
Usage notes: ISO specific field - best practices and we have for all.  They have recommended alternative AND new entries.  Needs remediation.
Crosswalk notes: All themekt="ISO 19115 Topic Category" go here

Label: Title
Usage notes: This is the main title of the work and we create it in-house.  Standard format is Place followed by feature description comm date.  Examples : "Idaho Tax Parcels, 2023" and "New York City Streets, 2002".  This is likely the most important element for discovery and should indicate what you will find in the data.  If there is a title upon receipt that needs to be significantly changed, may include in the Alternative Title field
Crosswalk notes: All xml records have one and only one entry here.  Remediated as of 7/2026

Label: WxS Identifier
Usage notes: Just the identifier for the WMS/WFS links which are appended in the References links.  Currently this is "sde:columbia." followed by ID
Crosswalk notes: Value should be "sde:columbia."  followed by distinfo/resdesc  Only include if access rights value is "public" otherwise blank
